additional_metadata.note: Inaugural Belgium dbt Meetup; location changed last-minute from original venue.

events.agenda: Inaugural Belgium dbt Meetup covering lessons learned running dbt in production, common pitfalls for data scientists adopting dbt, and OTA Insight's migration from ChartIO to Looker enabled by dbt.
events.event_description: First edition of the Analytics Engineering Belgium Meetup, bringing together professionals and enthusiasts interested in the latest developments in analytics engineering.
events.raw_text:
Thu, Mar 23, 2023 · 5:30 PM to 9:00 PM CET

De Hoorn, Sluisstraat 79, Leuven

Analytics Engineering Belgium Meetup #1

Schedule:
18h30: Lessons learned in the trenches with dbt (Mathias Lavaert - DPG Media)
19h00: dbt for data scientists - how to avoid the common pitfalls? (Grégoire Hornung - Qover)
19h45: How dbt Enabled OTA Insight's Seamless Migration from ChartIO to Looker (Lise Kerckhove - OTA Insight)

talks.description: Lessons learned using dbt in production workflows across multiple clients, covering common pitfalls, misconceptions, and lesser-known useful features.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 18h30: Lessons learned in the trenches with dbt (Mathias Lavaert - DPG Media)
talks.title: Lessons learned in the trenches with dbt

talks.description: Common challenges data scientists and aspiring analytics engineers face when building data infrastructure with dbt, illustrated with concrete situations from Qover.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 19h00: dbt for data scientists - how to avoid the common pitfalls? (Grégoire Hornung - Qover)
talks.title: dbt for data scientists - how to avoid the common pitfalls?

talks.description: How dbt enabled OTA Insight's migration from ChartIO to a self-service Looker platform in under 4 months, covering testing, validation, and avoiding vendor lock-in.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 19h45: How dbt Enabled OTA Insight's Seamless Migration from ChartIO to Looker (Lise Kerckhove - OTA Insight)
talks.speaker_title: Team Lead Analytics Engineering, OTA Insight
talks.title: How dbt Enabled OTA Insight's Seamless Migration from ChartIO to Looker

events.agenda: Second edition covering data mesh implementation with dbt, using DevContainers with dbt Core, and documenting KPIs beyond the dbt Semantic Layer.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks predominantly focused on community members' experience with dbt.
events.raw_text:
Wed, Jun 14, 2023 · 5:45 PM to 8:45 PM CEST

OTA Insight, Gaston Crommenlaan 6, Gent

Belgium dbt Meetup #2 (in-person)

Agenda:
18h30: Data Mesh with dbt (Charles Verleyen & Lukasz Sciga - Astrafy)
19h15: Boosting Collaboration and Productivity with DevContainers and dbt Core (Mateusz Marciniewicz - dataroots)
20h: Documenting KPIs: Beyond dbt Semantic Layer (Juan Manuel Perafan - GoDataDriven/Xebia)

talks.description: Demystifying the main concepts of data mesh and showing them in action with dbt using concrete data products, including downstream activation via the dbt semantic layer.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 18h30: Data Mesh with dbt (Charles Verleyen & Lukasz Sciga - Astrafy)
talks.title: Data Mesh with dbt

talks.description: Using DevContainers with dbt Core to provide a consistent development environment, simplifying collaboration and onboarding for data teams.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 19h15: Boosting Collaboration and Productivity with DevContainers and dbt Core (Mateusz Marciniewicz - dataroots)
talks.title: Boosting Collaboration and Productivity with DevContainers and dbt Core

talks.description: How to document KPIs beyond what the dbt Semantic Layer covers, including how metrics tie to business goals and their technical implementation.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 20h: Documenting KPIs: Beyond dbt Semantic Layer (Juan Manuel Perafan - GoDataDriven/Xebia)
talks.title: Documenting KPIs: Beyond dbt Semantic Layer

events.agenda: Third edition covering the shift from Spark to dbt-style tooling, developing analytics locally with DuckDB, and speeding up ML development with dbt Python models.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks predominantly focused on community members' experience with dbt.
events.location: Silversquare Delta, Av. Arnaud Fraiteur 15, Ixelles
events.raw_text:
Thu, Sep 28, 2023 · 5:45 PM to 9:30 PM CEST

Silversquare Delta, Ixelles

Belgium dbt Meetup #3 (in-person)

Agenda:
18h30: Why dbt will one day be bigger than Spark - Kris Peeters, CEO at Data Minded
19h15: Bring your own compute: benefits & pitfalls of developing analytics with DuckDB - Frederik Druyts, Consultant at Datashift
20h: Speed up ML development with python models in dbt - Dorian Van den Heede, Machine Learning Engineer at Dataroots

talks.description: Argument for why the data world is moving from distributed processing frameworks like Spark toward higher-level tooling like dbt.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 18h30: Why dbt will one day be bigger than Spark - Kris Peeters, CEO at Data Minded
talks.title: Why dbt will one day be bigger than Spark

talks.description: Using DuckDB to build, explore, and test data models locally, reducing the cost and complexity of analytics engineering work versus relying on distributed compute.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 19h15: Bring your own compute: benefits & pitfalls of developing analytics with DuckDB - Frederik Druyts, Consultant at Datashift
talks.title: Bring your own compute: benefits & pitfalls of developing analytics with DuckDB

talks.description: Do's and don'ts of using Python models within dbt for machine learning workflows, with design patterns to speed up development.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 20h: Speed up ML development with python models in dbt - Dorian Van den Heede, Machine Learning Engineer at Dataroots
talks.title: Speed up ML development with python models in dbt

talks.description: How dbt-core and its adapters are built under the hood, and how to make your first contributions to the open source project.

events.agenda: Fifth edition covering streamlining unit testing in dbt, modern data modeling strategies, and lowering BigQuery costs with dbt.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks predominantly focused on community members' experience with dbt.
events.location: Officenter Hasselt, Hendrik van Veldekesingel 150, Hasselt
events.raw_text:
Thu, Jan 25, 2024 · 5:30 PM to 9:30 PM CET

Officenter Hasselt, Hasselt

Belgium dbt Meetup #5 (in-person)

Presentations:
Streamlining unit testing in dbt: Increasing coverage with the least amount of effort - Wim Deca, Data Architect at Digital Hive
Reigniting Data Modeling: Sensible Strategies for the 2020s and Beyond - Niko Korvenlaita, CTO, Co-Founder at reconfigured
Using dbt to lower BigQuery costs - Rik Pauwels, Data Architect at Devoteam G Cloud

talks.description: How to streamline unit testing of dbt data transformations to increase test coverage with minimal effort.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Streamlining unit testing in dbt: Increasing coverage with the least amount of effort. Wim Deca, Data Architect at Digital Hive
talks.title: Streamlining unit testing in dbt: Increasing coverage with the least amount of effort

talks.description: Theoretical and practical lessons on productizing modern data modeling best practices into performant dbt code across major warehouses, covering incrementality, temporality, and event ledgers.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Reigniting Data Modeling: Sensible Strategies for the 2020s and Beyond. Niko Korvenlaita, CTO, Co-Founder at reconfigured
talks.title: Reigniting Data Modeling: Sensible Strategies for the 2020s and Beyond

talks.description: Techniques for lowering BigQuery costs using dbt, including cost guardrails, cost observability with job labels, and cost lowering via partitioning, clustering, and materialized views.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Using dbt to lower BigQuery costs. Rik Pauwels, Data Architect at Devoteam G Cloud
talks.title: Using dbt to lower BigQuery costs

Antwerp Port House, Antwerp

Presentations:

For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs - Bart van Delft, Solutions Architect, dbt Labs
